assumptions: The receivable arises from a dividend declared between entities within the same group and is already recognized.
The question is about hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.
The receivable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange exposure for at least one of the group entities.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 creates a specific exception for the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements when the resulting exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend receivable is an intragroup monetary item
The entities involved have different functional currencies
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation
Formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements are met
approaches.practical_implication: The FX risk on the receivable can be included in a documented hedge relationship only through this specific exception.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 allows foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to qualify in consolidated financial statements if exchange differences are not fully eliminated.
Paragraph 6.4.1 requires formal designation, documentation and satisfaction of hedge effectiveness criteria.

approaches.label: Default non-eligibility of intragroup items in consolidation
approaches.reasoning: The general IFRS 9 rule is that only exposures with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.
approaches.practical_implication: If the paragraph 6.3.6 exception is not met, the intragroup dividend receivable cannot be hedged in consolidation.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.5 excludes intragroup assets, liabilities and transactions from hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements, except for stated exceptions.

approaches.label: Risk-component designation of the receivable's FX risk
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 permits designation of a component of an item rather than the whole item, including a specific risk, if that component is separately identifiable and reliably measurable.
conditions: The underlying receivable is eligible as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements
The designated foreign currency risk component is separately identifiable
The designated foreign currency risk component is reliably measurable
Formal designation and effectiveness assessment are documented
approaches.practical_implication: If the receivable qualifies under the intragroup monetary-item exception, the entity may designate only its FX risk component.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.7 and paragraph B6.3.8 allow risk-component designation when the component is separately identifiable and reliably measurable.
Paragraph 6.4.1 requires documentation of the hedged item, hedged risk and hedge effectiveness assessment.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 separately permits certain highly probable forecast intragroup transactions, but the fact pattern here is a dividend already declared with a receivable recognized, not a forecast transaction.
conditions: The transaction is still forecast and highly probable
It is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into it
The foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss
approaches.practical_implication: This route is not the relevant basis for a recognized dividend receivable.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraphs 6.3.6, 6.5.2(b), B6.3.5 and B6.3.6 address qualifying forecast intragroup transactions that affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.reasoning: IFRIC 16 applies only to hedges of net investments in foreign operations and says it should not be applied by analogy to other hedge types.
conditions: The hedged item is an amount of net assets in a foreign operation included in the consolidated financial statements
approaches.practical_implication: An ordinary intragroup dividend receivable is not supported by IFRIC 16 as a separate hedge-accounting route.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraphs 7, 8 and 11 limit the Interpretation to net investment hedges and define the hedged item as an amount of net assets of the foreign operation.
